Operation 'DuplicatePreBuiltDesign' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Original summary is generic. Description is verbose and contains navigation references that don't clarify the core operation. Improved version clarifies: (1) what is being duplicated (pre-built design), (2) input expectations (optional body, optional name field), (3) default behavior (auto-naming convention), (4) output (new design object with unique ID), and (5) side effect (design stored in library). This helps AI agents understand when to use this operation and what to expect.

data.current_description:
**This endpoint allows you to duplicate one of the pre-built Twilio SendGrid designs**.

Like duplicating one of your existing designs, you are not required to pass any data in the body of a request to this endpoint. If you choose to leave the `name` field blank, your duplicate will be assigned the name of the design it was copied from with the text "Duplicate: " prepended to it. This name change is only a convenience, as the duplicate design will be assigned a unique ID that differentiates it from your other designs. You can retrieve the IDs for Twilio SendGrid pre-built designs using the "List SendGrid Pre-built Designs" endpoint.

You can modify your duplicate's name at the time of creation by passing an updated value to the `name` field when making the initial request.
More on retrieving design IDs can be found above.
data.summary_suggestion: Duplicate a pre-built design with optional name
data.description_suggestion: Creates a copy of a pre-built design from the Twilio SendGrid library. The request body is optional; if no `name` is provided, the duplicate will be named "Duplicate: [original name]". You can customize the duplicate's name by passing a `name` value in the request. The duplicated design receives a unique ID and is stored in your Design Library. Returns the newly created design object with its assigned ID and metadata.
data.reasoning: Original summary is generic. Description is verbose and contains navigation references that don't clarify the core operation. Improved version clarifies: (1) what is being duplicated (pre-built design), (2) input expectations (optional body, optional name field), (3) default behavior (auto-naming convention), (4) output (new design object with unique ID), and (5) side effect (design stored in library). This helps AI agents understand when to use this operation and what to expect.

message: Operation 'GetPreBuiltDesign' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_output_details, missing_context. Original summary lacks specificity about the ID parameter. Description is circular and vague about what data is returned. Improved version: (1) clarifies the required input (design ID), (2) specifies what is returned (design object with specific fields), (3) provides use case context (inspecting before duplication), and (4) uses consistent terminology with the API (Design Library, editor type, metadata). This enables AI agents to understand when this operation is needed and what information it provides.

data.current_description:
**This endpoint allows you to retrieve a single pre-built design**.

A GET request to `/designs/pre-builts/{id}` will retrieve details about a specific pre-built design.

This endpoint is valuable when retrieving details about a pre-built design that you wish to duplicate and modify.
data.summary_suggestion: Retrieve a pre-built design by ID
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves the full details of a specific pre-built design from the Twilio SendGrid library. Requires the pre-built design ID as a path parameter. Returns the design object including its name, editor type, thumbnail, and metadata. Use this operation to inspect a pre-built design before duplicating it or to view its structure and content.
data.reasoning: Original summary lacks specificity about the ID parameter. Description is circular and vague about what data is returned. Improved version: (1) clarifies the required input (design ID), (2) specifies what is returned (design object with specific fields), (3) provides use case context (inspecting before duplication), and (4) uses consistent terminology with the API (Design Library, editor type, metadata). This enables AI agents to understand when this operation is needed and what information it provides.

message: Operation 'ListDesign' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_output_details. Original summary 'List Designs' is too generic and doesn't distinguish this from pre-built designs. Description lacks clarity about the response structure and what fields are returned. Added specificity about user-created vs pre-built designs, pagination details, and response content to improve AI agent interpretability and help agents understand what data they'll receive.

data.current_description:
**This endpoint allows you to retrieve a list of designs already stored in your Design Library**.

A GET request to `/designs` will return a list of your existing designs. This endpoint will not return the pre-built Twilio SendGrid designs. Pre-built designs can be retrieved using the `/designs/pre-builts` endpoint, which is detailed below.

By default, you will receive 100 results per request; however, you can modify the number of results returned by passing an integer to the `page_size` query parameter.
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves a paginated list of designs stored in your Design Library. Returns only user-created designs, excluding pre-built Twilio SendGrid designs (use the `/designs/pre-builts` endpoint for those). Supports pagination via the `page_size` query parameter, defaulting to 100 results per request. Returns a 200 response containing an array of design summaries with metadata including design ID, name, creation date, and modification date.
data.reasoning: Original summary 'List Designs' is too generic and doesn't distinguish this from pre-built designs. Description lacks clarity about the response structure and what fields are returned. Added specificity about user-created vs pre-built designs, pagination details, and response content to improve AI agent interpretability and help agents understand what data they'll receive.

message: Operation 'DuplicateDesign' has semantic quality issues: missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Original description lacks clarity about required path parameter (design ID), optional input fields, and what the response contains. Missing information about side effects (original design unchanged, new unique ID assigned). Added specificity about input requirements, automatic naming behavior, response structure, and use case to improve AI agent understanding of when and how to use this operation.

data.current_description:
**This endpoint allows you to duplicate one of your existing designs**.

Modifying an existing design is often the easiest way to create something new.

You are not required to pass any data in the body of a request to this endpoint. If you choose to leave the `name` field blank, your duplicate will be assigned the name of the design it was copied from with the text "Duplicate: " prepended to it. This name change is only a convenience, as the duplicate will be assigned a unique ID that differentiates it from your other designs.

You can modify your duplicate's name at the time of creation by passing an updated value to the `name` field when making the initial request.
More on retrieving design IDs can be found below.
data.summary_suggestion: Duplicate existing design with optional rename
data.description_suggestion: Creates a copy of an existing design specified by its ID. Accepts optional DesignDuplicateInput with a custom name; if no name is provided, the duplicate is automatically named "Duplicate: [original name]". The duplicate receives a unique ID and is stored as a new design in your Design Library. Returns a 201 response with the newly created design object. The original design remains unchanged. Useful for creating variations of existing designs without modifying the original.
data.reasoning: Original description lacks clarity about required path parameter (design ID), optional input fields, and what the response contains. Missing information about side effects (original design unchanged, new unique ID assigned). Added specificity about input requirements, automatic naming behavior, response structure, and use case to improve AI agent understanding of when and how to use this operation.

message: Operation 'GetDesign' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_output_details. Original summary 'Get Design' is too generic. Description lacks specificity about what fields are returned and the response structure. Added clarity about the response schema (DesignOutput), specific fields returned, and practical use cases to help AI agents understand what data they'll receive and when this operation is appropriate.

data.current_description:
**This endpoint allows you to retrieve a single design**.

A GET request to `/designs/{id}` will retrieve details about a specific design in your Design Library.

This endpoint is valuable when retrieving information stored in a field that you wish to update using a PATCH request.
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves complete details for a specific design identified by its ID. Returns a 200 response containing the full DesignOutput object with all design properties including HTML content, metadata, editor type, and creation/modification timestamps. Useful for retrieving current design state before making updates via PATCH requests or for accessing design content for export or analysis.
data.reasoning: Original summary 'Get Design' is too generic. Description lacks specificity about what fields are returned and the response structure. Added clarity about the response schema (DesignOutput), specific fields returned, and practical use cases to help AI agents understand what data they'll receive and when this operation is appropriate.

message: Operation 'UpdateDesign' has semantic quality issues: missing_input_details, missing_output_details. Original description lacks clarity about which fields can be updated and what the response contains. Missing information about the response structure and return value. Added specificity about updatable fields, partial update behavior, response schema, and practical use cases to improve AI agent understanding of operation capabilities and effects.

data.current_description:
**This endpoint allows you to edit a design**.

The Design API supports PATCH requests, which allow you to make partial updates to a single design. Passing data to a specific field will update only the data stored in that field; all other fields will be unaltered.

For example, updating a design's name requires that you make a PATCH request to this endpoint with data specified for the `name` field only.

```
{
    "name": "<Updated Name>"
}
```
data.description_suggestion: Updates one or more properties of an existing design using a PATCH request. Supports partial updates - only provided fields are modified while all other fields remain unchanged. Accepts inline request body with updatable fields such as name and HTML content. Returns a 200 response with the updated DesignOutput object reflecting all changes. Useful for modifying design names, content, or metadata without affecting unspecified fields.
data.reasoning: Original description lacks clarity about which fields can be updated and what the response contains. Missing information about the response structure and return value. Added specificity about updatable fields, partial update behavior, response schema, and practical use cases to improve AI agent understanding of operation capabilities and effects.

Description lacks information about response status code and potential constraints (e.g., designs in use). Added clarity about permanence, response code, verification requirement, and potential limitations to help AI agents understand the severity and constraints of this destructive operation.

data.current_description:
**This endpoint allows you to delete a single design**.

Be sure to check the ID of the design you intend to delete before making this request; deleting a design is a permanent action.
data.description_suggestion: Permanently deletes a design from your Design Library identified by its ID. This action is irreversible - the design and all associated data are removed and cannot be recovered. Returns a 204 No Content response on successful deletion. Verify the design ID before executing this operation. Designs in use by active campaigns cannot be deleted.
data.reasoning: Original summary doesn't emphasize the permanent nature of the action. Description lacks information about response status code and potential constraints (e.g., designs in use). Added clarity about permanence, response code, verification requirement, and potential limitations to help AI agents understand the severity and constraints of this destructive operation.

message: Operation 'ListPreBuiltDesign' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_output_details. Original summary is somewhat verbose and doesn't clearly indicate pagination support. Description lacks specificity about response structure and fields returned. Added clarity about pagination, response schema, distinction from user designs, and practical use case to improve AI agent understanding of when to use this operation and what data will be returned.

data.current_description:
**This endpoint allows you to retrieve a list of pre-built designs provided by Twilio SendGrid**.

Unlike the `/designs` endpoint where *your* designs are stored, a GET request made to `designs/pre-builts` will retrieve a list of the pre-built Twilio SendGrid designs. This endpoint will not return the designs stored in your Design Library.

By default, you will receive 100 results per request; however, you can modify the number of results returned by passing an integer to the `page_size` query parameter.

This endpoint is useful for retrieving the IDs of Twilio SendGrid designs that you want to duplicate and modify.
data.summary_suggestion: Retrieve paginated list of pre-built designs
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves a paginated list of pre-built email designs provided by Twilio SendGrid. Returns only Twilio SendGrid templates, excluding user-created designs (use the `/designs` endpoint for those). Supports pagination via the `page_size` query parameter, defaulting to 100 results per request. Returns a 200 response containing an array of design summaries with metadata. Useful for discovering template designs to duplicate and customize for your campaigns.
data.reasoning: Original summary is somewhat verbose and doesn't clearly indicate pagination support. Description lacks specificity about response structure and fields returned. Added clarity about pagination, response schema, distinction from user designs, and practical use case to improve AI agent understanding of when to use this operation and what data will be returned.
